key_insight: Domain-specific biomedical transformer embeddings, especially BioBERT, improved detection of dosing-error risk in clinical trial protocols compared with a ClinicalBERT baseline.
clinical_relevance: A structured text-plus-metadata screening approach could help flag trials with elevated dosing-error risk earlier, supporting safety monitoring and review workflows.
method: Encoded clinical trial text with ClinicalBERT, PubMedBERT, BioBERT, and MedCPT
Combined embeddings with categorical metadata features
Compared classical ML and neural models, including logistic regression, gradient boosting, SVC, and residual networks
evidence_signal: Using ROC-AUC for evaluation, BioBERT reached 0.794 with logistic regression, and the best models achieved ROC-AUCs from 0.821 to 0.853.
watch_item: Results are based on model benchmarking rather than prospective deployment, so real-world performance and generalizability still need validation.
paper_title: CaresAI at CT-DEB26: Detecting Dosing Errors In Clinical Trials Using Domain-Specific Transformer Embeddings and Classification Models
